user_query:
I'm doing a school project on our town's history and want to include a small, fact-based public health analysis from the materials I already have. Please use the local files in input/ to do this and produce two outputs under output/.

What I need:
1) Create and run a local script (save it as scripts/compute_trends.py) that reads input/hospital_admissions.csv and input/town_population.csv, and writes a summary CSV to output/health_trends.csv with these columns exactly: condition, decade, total_admissions_decade, peak_year, peak_admissions, rate_per_1000_rounded. Constraints for the CSV:
- Only analyze the two conditions: Influenza and Tuberculosis.
- Group admissions by decade labeled as, for example, "1910s", "1930s" (based on the year’s decade).
- For each condition, compute:
  • decade: the decade with the highest total admissions for that condition (sum within that decade).
  • total_admissions_decade: the total admissions for that condition within the highest decade.
  • peak_year: the single year with the highest admissions for that condition across all years (if there’s a tie, choose the earliest year).
  • peak_admissions: admissions count in that peak year.
  • rate_per_1000_rounded: (peak_admissions / population_in_peak_year) * 1000, rounded to 2 decimals using population values from input/town_population.csv.
- Save the generated CSV to output/health_trends.csv.

2) Update the draft report at input/report_draft.md and save the updated version to output/report_final.md by replacing the placeholder line [[INSERT-TRENDS-HERE]] under the "## Health Trends" header with:
- A single 1–2 sentence overview naming, for each of the two conditions, which decade had the most admissions.
- Then, for each condition (Influenza and Tuberculosis), include a short block listing:
  - Condition: <name>
  - Highest decade: <decade label>
  - Peak year: <year> with admissions <N> and rate per 1000 <rounded to 2 decimals>
  - Reference: <exact sentence from input/newspaper_clippings.txt that contains both the condition name and the peak year>
The "Reference" sentence must be copied verbatim from input/newspaper_clippings.txt and must include both the condition word and the peak year.

Files to use:
- input/hospital_admissions.csv (admissions by year and condition)
- input/town_population.csv (population by year, for rate calculations)
- input/newspaper_clippings.txt (for the reference sentences)
- input/report_draft.md (to be updated; save the updated version to output/report_final.md)

Deliverables:
- scripts/compute_trends.py (the script you wrote and ran)
- output/health_trends.csv (computed summary as specified)
- output/report_final.md (the updated report with the filled-in Health Trends section)

Please keep paths and column names exact so I can grade this alongside my sources. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
